title: Colonel Karl Nell on the evolution of life on earth & faster than light travel #space #earth #aliens
summary: Colonel Karl Nell discusses evidence suggesting life evolved on Earth at the earliest possible time and challenges common assumptions about the rarity of life and impossibility of faster-than-light travel. He cites fossilized blue-green algae, recent microbe discoveries, and the possibility of panspermia.

events.description: Scientists identified microbes in stasis in two billion year old terrestrial rock that were brought back to life

key_claims.claim: Blue-green algae fossilized in stromatolites date from 3.5 billion years ago
Recent scholarship indicates life may have evolved as early as 4.1 billion years ago
Earth is approximately 5 billion years old
There is no evidence that life is uncommon or that faster than light travel is impossible
Microbes can live in outer space and remain viable
Microbes in stasis in two billion year old terrestrial rock were recently brought back to life
Evidence suggests life either evolved very quickly on Earth or panspermia occurred
